data.title: Ethereum spot ETF had a total net inflow of $4.6817 million yesterday, and none of the nine ETFs had a net outflow
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (March 28, Eastern Time), the total net inflow of Ethereum spot ETF was 4.6817 million US dollars.
The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest net inflow in a single day yesterday was Grayscale ETF ETHE, with a single-day net inflow of 4.6817 million US dollars. Currently, the total net outflow of ETHE has reached 4.167 billion US dollars.
As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETF is 6.425 billion US dollars, and the ETF net asset ratio (market value to the total market value of Ethereum) is 2.84%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached 2.409 billion US dollars.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs had a total net outflow of $93.1572 million yesterday, and none of the twelve ETFs had a net inflow
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (March 28, Eastern Time), the total net outflow of Bitcoin spot ETFs was US$93.1572 million.
The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest net outflow in a single day yesterday was Fidelity Bitcoin ETF FBTC, with a single-day net outflow of US$93.1572 million. Currently, FBTC's total net inflow has reached US$11.469 billion.
As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs was US$94.388 billion, and the ETF net asset ratio (market value to the total market value of Bitcoin) reached 5.68%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached US$36.245 billion.

data.title: Amber Premium Celebrates Nasdaq Listing with Closing Bell Ceremony
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Amber Premium, a subsidiary of Amber Group, marked its successful listing on the Nasdaq by ringing the closing bell at the Nasdaq MarketSite in Times Square, New York, on March 28. The company officially began trading on Nasdaq on March 13, becoming the first Singapore-based crypto company to list on the exchange and one of the few Asian crypto firms to achieve this milestone.

Amber Premium CEO Wayne Huo stated that the ceremony symbolizes the company's entry into a new phase of development, with plans to continue global expansion, strengthen infrastructure, and collaborate with clients to create the next generation of institutional-grade crypto financial services. Amber Group CEO and Amber Premium Chairman Michael Wu also emphasized the company's commitment to expanding its business while focusing on compliance, transparency, and customer-oriented innovation to advance the digital asset industry.

data.title: FTX to pay back its major creditors by the end of May
data.description:
FTX, a former cryptocurrency firm, will start paying its creditors on May 30, according to lawyer Andrew Dietderich. The company has accumulated $11. 4 billion in cash since it shut down and filed for bankruptcy in November 2022. Minor creditors, who hold what FTX labels “convenience claims,” began receiving payments earlier, but the main group of […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ftx-pay-back-major-creditors/

data.title: Ethereum whales transferred 16,800 ETH to Binance in the past 16 hours
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News On-chain analyst @ai_9684xtpa monitored that the address 0xcD4...364D6 recharged 16,800 ETH (about 31.62 million US dollars) to Binance in the past 16 hours, suspected of selling. In the past week, the whale has transferred a total of 31,002 ETH (about 59.97 million US dollars) to the exchange, with an average recharge price of 1,934 US dollars.

data.title: Vitalik: There should be less discussion about public product funding and more focus on open source funding
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Vitalik Buterin published an article "We should talk less about public goods funding and more about open source funding", proposing that in the digital ecosystem, more attention should be paid to "open source funding" rather than "public product funding".
Vitalik pointed out that the term "public goods" is often misunderstood as "goods provided by the government" and is easily abused, while "open source" has a clear definition and is more suitable as the core standard for digital public goods funding. The article emphasizes that many recognized digital public goods (such as open source software, blockchain research, etc.) are open source, so focusing funding on "open source" can avoid the generalization and abuse of the concept of "public goods"
He further stated that funding for open source projects should not be just because of their "open source" attributes, but to support open source projects that are truly valuable to mankind. This is also the core issue that the public product funding mechanism should focus on.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 610 USDT with a 3.55% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 29, 2025, 03:12 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 610 USDT and is now trading at 609.929993 USDT, with a narrowed 3.55% decrease in 24 hours.
